mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly suppressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC exceeds 0.05 or positive 20-day returns dominate when FEDFUNDS remains above 4.0%.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid three-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeds the 0.2 percentage point threshold, signaling an imminent labor market deterioration that forces equity market repricing downward.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand expansion, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST yields negative forward returns in > 55% of cases over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained upward trend in the index of industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing and utility output, generating positive short-term equity momentum.
failure_condition: INDPRO uptrends produce negative 5-day forward returns in > 60% of observations during easing periods.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation exceeds the 315.0 index threshold while the federal funds rate remains above 4.0%, the resulting stagflationary pressure compresses equity valuations.
failure_condition: The joint condition of high CPIAUCSL and elevated FEDFUNDS yields positive forward returns in > 50% of triggered months.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month gains in total nonfarm payrolls indicate robust labor market expansion, supporting corporate revenue growth and generating positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: Positive PAYEMS growth correlates with negative 60-day forward returns with a Rank IC below 0.02 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth falling below the 1.5 percent quarterly threshold signals severe demand contraction, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: GDP growth below 1.5% is followed by positive 20-day returns in > 70% of historical occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between declining industrial production and rising unemployment confirms a broad supply-side contraction, forcing equity markets to price in recessionary losses.
failure_condition: The joint signal of falling INDPRO and rising UNRATE produces positive forward returns in > 55% of cases over a 5-year backtest.
